summary: C‑Miller remastered soundboard recording is solid, but the 1989 show is considered average and the player tracklist is off by one.
review: The recording benefits from a clean Miller remaster and delivers the usual clear soundboard quality, with no major audio flaws noted. However reviewers feel the performance itself falls short of the stronger 1989 classics, offering nothing particularly memorable. A minor inconvenience is that the tracklisting on the player is misaligned by one position.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is judged below typical 1989 highlights (≈3/5) while recording quality is praised as good/excellent (≈4.5/5). Weighted combination (70% show, 30% recording) yields a rating around 3.5.

recording_quality.technical_notes: C‑Miller remaster provides clear, well‑balanced audio; no significant dropouts or muddiness reported. The only technical complaint is a tracklisting offset on the player.

show_quality.setlist_flow: No specific comments on set pacing; reviewers simply note the overall performance is not as strong as other 1989 shows.
